book_claims.claim: Red beans are listed in the Longevity Diet as a calcium source (25 mg per 1/2 cup cooked, ~3% DV) and a plant protein source, recommended at approximately 1/2 cup cooked per serving.
book_claims.mechanism: Provide plant protein and modest calcium; as legumes, they are a longevity-associated food across all documented centenarian populations, supporting gut microbiome health, blood sugar regulation, and cardiovascular protection.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Luo et al., Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry (2010) — PMID 20143790
supplementary_research.finding: Red adzuki beans are exceptionally rich in anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins (up to 200 mg/100 g cooked), polyphenols with potent antioxidant activity shown to reduce LDL oxidation and inflammation in cell and animal studies — mechanisms central to longevity.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Bazzano et al., Archives of Internal Medicine (2001) — PMID 11176744
supplementary_research.finding: Legume consumption 4+ times per week vs. less than once weekly associated with 22% lower coronary heart disease risk in 9,632 US adults; the class benefit applies to all legume varieties including red beans.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Sone et al., Journal of Ethnopharmacology (2019) — PMID 30902714
supplementary_research.finding: Adzuki bean polysaccharides and polyphenols demonstrated hepatoprotective effects and improved lipid profiles in animal models; in Japanese dietary surveys, adzuki bean consumption correlates with lower metabolic syndrome prevalence.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Complete amino acid profile when paired with grains; PDCAAS ~0.75; particularly rich in lysine, a limiting amino acid in grains.
Primarily in seed coat; partially survives cooking; red/purple color intensity is a visual proxy for polyphenol content.
Important for DNA methylation and homocysteine metabolism; cooking reduces folate by ~40% vs. raw; retain cooking water when possible.
High RS content supports favorable gut microbiome composition; increases further when cooked and cooled.

synergies.description: Classic complementary protein pairing across Asian and Latin American cuisines; together they provide a complete essential amino acid profile.
Vitamin C from peppers enhances non-heme iron absorption from red beans, critical for maintaining iron adequacy on plant-dominant longevity diets.
Olive oil polyphenols and monounsaturated fats complement the legume's protein and fiber, creating a broadly anti-inflammatory meal.
